label: _MEASURE_JugglerBeg_TOOLTIP_+0
source: The easiest way of getting money is still begging. Send your jugglers out to ask the townsfolk for alms, but don't rely on the benevolence of the citizens alone. Offer the people some entertainment and music to elicit bigger donations from them. But note that the citizens are less inclined to give away some of their wealth during wars and epidemics.

label: _MEASURE_LayTarot_TOOLTIP_+0
source: Use the curiosity and superstition of the common people to woo coins out of their purses and offer to read tarot cards for the townsfolk. Not many of them will be able to resist a game of fate and they're only too willing to disregard an unlucky card.$NA generally popular game with moderate income.

label: _MEASURE_TELLFORTUNE_TOOLTIP_+0
source: In these dismal times, where one wrong step can easily spell hunger and misery, everyone is looking for advice and assurance. Some seek solace in the arms of Mother Church. Others, however, you may be able to give good advice through the prophetic abilities of your workers – it will fill your pockets at least, and your people will have long since flown by the time the gullible finally realise that their futures aren’t quite what they’d expected…

label: _NETWORK_MAP_DESC_ULM_+0
source: At the end of the 14th Century, the city councillors decided to expand Ulm to quadruple its original size. In the course of this process, many craftsmen and merchants took to the city, along with many an unsavoury character, to partake in some of the new prosperity.

label: _MEASURE_MineGuards_TOOLTIP_+0
source: Are you tired of nefarious ruffians robbing your mine, setting it on fire, or even capturing it? With this action, you will recruit guards who will then dedicate themselves entirely to your - yes your mine. And the ruffians who try to attack it.
current: Are you tired of nefarious ruffians robbing your mine, setting it on fire, or even capturing it? With this action, you will recruit guards who will then dedicate themselves entirely to your - yes your mine. And the ruffians who try to attack it.

label: _MEASURE_ToggleBuildingStatistics_TOOLTIP_+0
source: Overview the economy linked to this building such as the items sold and bought by this building, including the materials required to produce them accessible by the chain of production.

label: _MEASURE_ReflectHeritage_TOOLTIP_+0
source: Sit upon the ancestral seat and contemplate the legacy of your dynasty. Those who have achieved true greatness may find something extraordinary.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_MUSING_+0
source: Let me look upon all that we have built...

label: _REFLECTHERIT_FAIL_+0
source: There is still much left to accomplish... The walls whisper of unfulfilled potential.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_FAIL_+1
source: I sense an incompleteness. This palace must first become worthy of a true legacy.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_FAIL_+2
source: Not yet... The echoes of destiny demand more of me.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_SUCCESS_+0
source: At last... I feel the weight of centuries of ambition crystallized in this very moment. This dynasty shall be eternal!

label: _REFLECTHERIT_SUCCESS_+1
source: From nothing, an empire. From dust, a dynasty that shall outlive the stars themselves.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_MUSING_+1
source: The blood of generations courses through these halls... What secrets do these stones hold?

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_+0
source: Every challenge conquered. Every foe vanquished. Every ambition fulfilled. I stand as the living legend of this world!

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_+1
source: Let the heavens bear witness! No mortal dynasty has ever risen so high. We are beyond history itself!

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_+2
source: What is this? An ancient chronicle, hidden beneath the family seal...

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_+3
source: Page after page of victories and conquests... not a single chapter left unfinished.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_+4
source: From these walls I have watched a dynasty shape the very fate of this land... and now its story is complete.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_GUEST_+0
source: My lord, the halls have never shone brighter. Truly, this dynasty stands above all others.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_GUEST_+1
source: Indeed! Even the merchants in distant lands speak of this house with reverence.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_TOAST_+0
source: To the dynasty! May its glory endure for a thousand years!

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_NOBLE_+0
source: My lord... what that scroll contains... it speaks of a dynasty that has conquered every challenge known to this world.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_NOBLE_+1
source: We have all witnessed it with our own eyes. There is no greater house in all the realm.

label: _REFLECTHERIT_ULTRA_SWORD_+0
source: By this blade and the blood of my ancestors - I claim what no dynasty has ever claimed! Absolute mastery over every trial, every conquest, every ambition!

label: _REFLECTHERIT_MUSING_+2
source: And now, sitting upon this seat of power, I wonder... have I truly achieved all that I set out to do?

label: _REFLECTHERIT_SUCCESS_+2
source: Every stone of this castle, every coin in our vaults, every title we bear -- all of it, proof that this dynasty is destined for eternity.

Combat Basics
Combat in the world is dangerous but sometimes necessary. Whether defending your {tip:CART}carts{/tip} from bandits or fighting rivals in the streets, understanding combat is essential.
Key Stats

Hiring Guards
You can hire guards to protect your buildings and {tip:CART}carts{/tip}. Guards fight automatically when threats approach. Better equipment means better protection.
When to Fight
[type="bullet"] Defend your trade routes from bandits and rival attacks. Protect your buildings from arson and sabotage. [link="Measure_EscortCharacterOrTransport"]Escort important characters through dangerous areas. label: Guides/Court.txt
source:
<header>The Court System</header>

<text>
The {tip:COURT}court{/tip} is where disputes are settled and justice is served. Use it wisely to bring down your rivals - or defend yourself against their accusations.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>How Court Works</header>

<list>
  [type="numbered"]
  <item><text>[link="Measure_OrderCollectEvidence"]Gather {tip:EVIDENCE}evidence{/tip} against your target through espionage.</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="Measure_ChargeCharacter"]File an accusation at the courthouse.</text></item>
  <item><text>Present your evidence before the jury.</text></item>
  <item><text>The jury votes based on evidence strength and their {tip:FAVOR}favor{/tip} toward each party.</text></item>
  <item><text>If found guilty, the accused faces fines, imprisonment, or worse.</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>Influencing the Jury</header>

<text>
Jury members are influenced by their personal {tip:FAVOR}favor{/tip} toward the accused and accuser. Building relationships with prominent citizens before a trial can tip the scales in your favor.
</text>

label: Guides/Economy.txt
source:
<header>Economy Overview</header>

<text>
The economy is the foundation of your {tip:DYNASTY}dynasty's{/tip} power. Without {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip}, you cannot build, hire, or influence.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Sources of Income</header>

<list>
  [type="numbered"]
  <item><text>Production - Your buildings produce goods that can be sold at the market.</text></item>
  <item><text>Trading - Buy low and sell high using {tip:CART}carts{/tip} to transport goods between markets.</text></item>
  <item><text>Political Income - Holding {tip:OFFICE}offices{/tip} grants a regular salary.</text></item>
  <item><text>Special Actions - Various {tip:MEASURE}measures{/tip} can generate income through less conventional means.</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>Trade Routes</header>

<text>
Goods are produced in your buildings and must be transported to market stalls for sale. Each {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip} has its own marketplace with supply and demand. Prices fluctuate based on availability.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<text>[link="Trading"]Learn more about trading strategies >></text>

label: Guides/FirstSteps.txt
source:
<header>Your First Steps</header>

<text>
Congratulations on founding your {tip:DYNASTY}dynasty{/tip}! Here is what you should focus on in the early game.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Step 1: Build Your First Business</header>

<text>
Your starting {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip} should be invested into a building. Which building depends on your guild:
</text>

<list>
  [type="bullet"]
  <item><text>[link="Guilds"]{tip:PATRON_GUILD}Patrons{/tip} - Start with an Inn or a Farm for steady income.</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="Guilds"]{tip:ARTISAN_GUILD}Artisans{/tip} - Build a Smithy to produce weapons and {tip:ITEM_Tool}tools{/tip}.</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="Guilds"]{tip:SCHOLAR_GUILD}Scholars{/tip} - Establish a Church or Hospital for healing services.</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="Guilds"]{tip:ROGUE_GUILD}Rogues{/tip} - Open a Tavern for quick profits.</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>Step 2: Hire Workers</header>

<text>
Your building needs workers to produce goods. Hire employees from the population. More skilled workers produce at better rates.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Step 3: Start Trading</header>

<text>
Purchase a {tip:CART}cart{/tip} to transport goods from your building to the marketplace. Keep an eye on market prices in each {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip}.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Step 4: Build Your Reputation</header>

<text>
With enough fame, you can pursue {tip:NOBILITY}nobility titles{/tip} and eventually run for {tip:OFFICE}political office{/tip}. You raise your limit of workshops as you obtain new titles.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<text>[link="Economy"]Read the Economy Overview >></text>

label: Guides/Guilds.txt
source:
<header>Guild Masters and The Alderman</header>

<text>
This page lacks information for now, so be mindful of this warning.
</text>

<text>
Each {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip} has four guilds, each led by an elected {tip:GUILD_MASTER}Guild Master{/tip}. Above them all sits the {tip:ALDERMAN}Alderman{/tip}.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>The Four Guilds</header>

<text>[r=160][g=100][b=20]Patron Guild</text>
<text>
The Patrons work the land and offer services. The {tip:GUILD_PATRON_MASTER}Patron Guild Master{/tip} takes care of inns, farms and more. To become one, you must own businesses and become respectable in the field.
</text>

<text>[r=130][g=70][b=20]Artisan Guild</text>
<text>
The Artisans are craftsmen and builders. The {tip:GUILD_ARTISAN_MASTER}Artisan Guild Master{/tip} sets the standard for quality goods. Smithies, jewellries and more fall under their domain.
</text>

<text>[r=30][g=60][b=140]Scholar Guild</text>
<text>
The Scholars pursue knowledge and spiritual guidance. The {tip:GUILD_SCHOLAR_MASTER}Scholar Guild Master{/tip} oversees churches, hospitals, and places of learning. Healing and alchemy are also their specialties.
</text>

<text>[r=120][g=25][b=20]Rogue Guild</text>
<text>
The Rogues operate in the shadows. The {tip:GUILD_ROGUE_MASTER}Rogue Guild Master{/tip} controls taverns, gambling dens, and less savory enterprises. While frowned upon, their influence is undeniable.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>The Alderman</header>

<text>
The {tip:ALDERMAN}Alderman{/tip} is elected from among the most prominent guild masters. They can:
</text>

<list>
  [type="bullet"]
  <item><text>[link="Measure_ImposeASalesFreezeAlderman"]Impose sales freezes on rival businesses</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<text>[link="Politics"]Learn more about the political system >></text>

label: Guides/Measures.txt
source:
<header>Measures Overview</header>

<text>
Measures are the actions your characters can perform. Every interaction in the world, from hiring an {tip:EMPLOYEE}employee{/tip} to brewing a potion, from filing a lawsuit to seducing a rival, is a measure. Measures cost time, and some cost {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip}.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Categories</header>

<text>
This guide documents the measures used by the entire game. Not all are available to every character; class, {tip:NOBILITY}nobility title{/tip}, current {tip:OFFICE}office{/tip}, and situation determine what your characters can do.
</text>

<list>
  [type="bullet"]
  <item><text>[link="MeasuresBuilding"]Building & Economy</text><text> - Building management, employees, production, housing, religion</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="MeasuresFamily"]Dialogue & Romance</text><text> - Social interactions, courtship, marriage</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="MeasuresAggressive"]Aggressive & Rogue</text><text> - Combat, theft, sabotage, intrigue</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="MeasuresPrivilege"]Office Privileges</text><text> - Actions unlocked by political offices</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="MeasuresFamilyGroup"]Family & Group</text><text> - Inventory, party, reputation, trade routes</text></item>
  <item><text>[link="MeasuresRPG"]RPG Actions</text><text> - Role-playing and divehouse measures</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>General Notes</header>

<list>
  [type="bullet"]
  <item><text>Each measure has an interrupt priority. Higher priorities can override current activities.</text></item>
  <item><text>Some measures grant {tip:EXPERIENCE}experience{/tip} to the character performing them.</text></item>
  <item><text>Many measures require a target: another character, a building, a cart, or a location.</text></item>
  <item><text>Characters can queue multiple measures in sequence.</text></item>
</list>

source:
<header>Artefact Measures</header>

<text>
Artefacts are special items that grant unique effects when used. They are sometimes rare and powerful, ranging from simple luck charms to devastating tools of sabotage.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Using Artefacts</header>

<text>
Artefacts are consumed or triggered via the measure system. Each item has its own usage rules: some affect the current character, some target another character, and some influence an area. Many provide temporary bonuses to skills or attributes.
</text>

label: Guides/Offices.txt
source:
<header>Settlement Offices</header>

<text>
Each {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip} has a hierarchy of {tip:OFFICE}offices{/tip} that govern the town. As the settlement grows, more offices become available - from minor positions like the Watchman or the Bailiff up through the Mayor, the Chief Judge, the Marshal, the Bishop, and at the very top of the realm, the King.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Office Access Rights</header>

<text>
To hold an office, your character must have the appropriate {tip:NOBILITY}nobility title{/tip}. Higher offices require higher titles, which in turn require more {tip:FAME}fame{/tip} and {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip}.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Office Benefits</header>

<list>
  [type="bullet"]
  <item><text>Regular salary.</text></item>
  <item><text>Unique {tip:MEASURE}measures{/tip} only available to office holders.</text></item>
  <item><text>Increased {tip:FAME}fame{/tip}.</text></item>
  <item><text>Influence over town policies and laws.</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>The settlement office hierarchy</header>

<text>

label: Guides/Politics.txt
source:
<header>The Political System</header>

<text>
Power in the world is not just about {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip} - it is about holding {tip:OFFICE}offices{/tip}, {tip:FAME}fame{/tip}, and earning the {tip:FAVOR}favor{/tip} of influential people.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Office Hierarchy</header>

<text>
Each {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip} has a hierarchy of offices. As your settlement grows, higher offices become available.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>How to Get Elected</header>

<list>
  [type="numbered"]
  <item><text>Earn a {tip:NOBILITY}nobility title{/tip} matching the office level requirement.</text></item>
  <item><text>Improve {tip:FAVOR}favor{/tip} with voters through gifts and kind deeds.</text></item>
  <item><text>Apply for the office during the election period.</text></item>
  <item><text>Wait for the election session - voters will cast their ballots.</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>Benefits of Office</header>

<text>
Holding an office grants your character regular income, political privileges, and enhanced prestige. Some offices allow you to influence laws, impose taxes, and more.
</text>

<text>[link="Offices"]See all settlement offices >></text>
current:
<header>The Political System</header>

<text>
Power in the world is not just about {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip} - it is about holding {tip:OFFICE}offices{/tip}, {tip:FAME}fame{/tip}, and earning the {tip:FAVOR}favor{/tip} of influential people.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>Office Hierarchy</header>

<text>
Each {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip} has a hierarchy of offices. As your settlement grows, higher offices become available.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>How to Get Elected</header>

<list>
  [type="numbered"]
  <item><text>Earn a {tip:NOBILITY}nobility title{/tip} matching the office level requirement.</text></item>
  <item><text>Improve {tip:FAVOR}favor{/tip} with voters through gifts and kind deeds.</text></item>
  <item><text>Apply for the office during the election period.</text></item>
  <item><text>Wait for the election session - voters will cast their ballots.</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>Benefits of Office</header>

<text>
Holding an office grants your character regular income, political privileges, and enhanced prestige. Some offices allow you to influence laws, impose taxes, and more.
</text>

<text>[link="Offices"]See all settlement offices >></text>

label: Guides/Trading.txt
source:
<header>Trading Guide</header>

<text>
Trading is the lifeblood of commerce. Master it, and {tip:GOLD}gold{/tip} will flow into your coffers.
</text>

<separator></separator>

<header>The Trading Cycle</header>

<list>
  [type="numbered"]
  <item><text>Your buildings produce goods when staffed with workers.</text></item>
  <item><text>Load goods onto a {tip:CART}cart{/tip} at your building.</text></item>
  <item><text>Send the cart to a marketplace in any {tip:SETTLEMENT}settlement{/tip}.</text></item>
  <item><text>Sell goods at the market stall. Prices depend on supply and demand.</text></item>
  <item><text>Repeat!</text></item>
</list>

<separator></separator>

<header>Trading Tips</header>

<list>
  [type="bullet"]
  <item><text>Check prices across different settlements - some pay more for certain goods.</text></item>
  <item><text>Upgrade your carts for faster transport and larger capacity.</text></item>
  <item><text>Guard your carts on dangerous routes to prevent bandit raids.</text></item>
  <item><text>Buy raw materials cheaply and sell finished goods at a premium.</text></item>
</list>
